articles.title: Nemotron-Cascade 2: Post-Training LLMs with Cascade RL and Multi-Domain On-Policy Distillation
articles.summary: Introduces Nemotron-Cascade 2, a 30B MoE model with 3B activated parameters delivering best-in-class reasoning and strong agentic capabilities. Despite its compact size, it approaches frontier open models in mathematical and coding performance and achieves Gold Medal-level performance in the 2025 International Mathematical Olympiad.

articles.title: Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro
articles.summary: A 32B parameter flagship LLM designed for enterprise-grade complexity through reasoning-focused architecture. Targets Korean-language and domain-specific scenarios where scaling alone is insufficient.

articles.title: F2LLM-v2: Inclusive, Performant, and Efficient Embeddings for a Multilingual World
articles.summary: A new family of multilingual embedding models in 8 sizes from 80M to 14B parameters, supporting over 200 languages. Trained on 60 million high-quality data samples with emphasis on previously underserved mid- and low-resource languages.

articles.title: OpenAI is planning a desktop 'superapp'
articles.summary: OpenAI is working on a desktop superapp that merges its ChatGPT app, Codex AI coding app, and AI-powered Atlas browser into one application. The company aims to simplify its various product efforts to address fragmentation issues that have been slowing development.

articles.title: OpenAI is throwing everything into building a fully automated researcher
articles.summary: OpenAI has refocused its research efforts toward building an AI researcher - a fully automated agent-based system capable of tackling large, complex problems independently. This represents a new grand challenge for the San Francisco firm, shifting from current capabilities to autonomous research systems.

articles.title: OpenAI is acquiring open source Python tool-maker Astral
articles.summary: OpenAI is acquiring Astral, the maker of popular Python tools including uv and ruff. The Codex maker says it will continue to support these open source projects after the deal closes.

articles.title: Rethinking open source mentorship in the AI era
articles.summary: As contribution volume grows, mentorship signals are harder to read. The 3 Cs framework helps maintainers mentor more strategically without burning out.

articles.title: How Squad runs coordinated AI agents inside your repository
articles.summary: An inside look at repository-native orchestration with GitHub Copilot and the design patterns behind multi-agent workflows that stay inspectable, predictable, and collaborative.

articles.title: Amazon is making an Alexa phone
articles.summary: Over ten years after shelving the Fire Phone, Amazon is reportedly planning to launch another smartphone code-named "Transformer" that will center around Amazon's AI assistant Alexa. The phone is currently in development in Amazon's ZeroOne group led by former Microsoft executive J Allard.

articles.title: Why people really hate AI
articles.summary: There's a big disconnect between companies hunting for places to deploy AI and people's actual response to the technology. Study after study shows people are worried about AI's effects and don't find it worth the downsides, despite corporate enthusiasm.

articles.title: Valve's huge SteamOS 3.8 update adds long-awaited features — and supports Steam Machine
articles.summary: Valve has released SteamOS 3.8.0 in preview, the first release to support the upcoming Steam Machine living room gaming PC. It includes long-awaited features for handhelds and expanded support for other companies' devices including Microsoft's Xbox Ally series and Lenovo Legion Go 2.

articles.title: The best AI investment might be in energy tech
articles.summary: Power has become one of the biggest bottlenecks in rolling out new AI data centers, creating an opening for investors in energy technology solutions.

articles.title: Jeff Bezos reportedly wants $100 billion to buy and transform old manufacturing firms with AI
articles.summary: The Amazon magnate has a new project centered around acquiring industrial firms and revamping them with AI technology, reportedly seeking $100 billion in funding for the initiative.

articles.title: Online bot traffic will exceed human traffic by 2027, Cloudflare CEO says
articles.summary: AI bots may outnumber humans online by 2027 according to Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince, as generative AI agents dramatically increase web traffic and infrastructure demands.

articles.title: Continually self-improving AI
articles.summary: Modern language model-based AI systems are capped by their human creators in three key ways: data-inefficient knowledge acquisition, reliance on finite human-generated data, and limited ability for self-improvement. This work explores pathways for AI systems to overcome these fundamental limitations.

articles.title: Multi-Trait Subspace Steering to Reveal the Dark Side of Human-AI Interaction
articles.summary: Recent incidents have highlighted alarming cases where human-AI interactions led to negative psychological outcomes including mental health crises and user harm. This study investigates the mechanisms underlying harmful human-AI interactions and presents methods for studying these risks systematically.

articles.title: From Weak Cues to Real Identities: Evaluating Inference-Driven De-Anonymization in LLM Agents
articles.summary: LLM-based agents can autonomously reconstruct real-world identities from scattered, individually non-identifying cues by combining sparse information with public data. This study demonstrates a growing privacy risk that may weaken traditional anonymization barriers.

articles.title: DEAF: A Benchmark for Diagnostic Evaluation of Acoustic Faithfulness in Audio Language Models
articles.summary: Recent Audio Multimodal Large Language Models show impressive performance on speech benchmarks, yet it remains unclear whether they genuinely process acoustic signals or rely on text-based semantic inference. This work introduces DEAF, a benchmark of over 2,700 conflict stimuli to systematically study this question.

articles.title: Don't Vibe Code, Do Skele-Code: Interactive No-Code Notebooks for Subject Matter Experts to Build Lower-Cost Agentic Workflows
articles.summary: Skele-Code is a natural-language and graph-based interface for building workflows with AI agents, designed for non-technical users. It supports incremental, interactive development where agents are only used for code generation and error recovery, not orchestration.

articles.title: Thoughts on OpenAI acquiring Astral and uv/ruff/ty
articles.summary: Community discussion and analysis of OpenAI's acquisition of Astral, the company behind popular Python development tools including uv, ruff, and other widely-used open source projects.
